title: Xtra Energizing Japanese Bryndza Cheese Takikomi Gohan
description: A bitter and bouncy Japanese dish featuring bryndza cheese, steam, and anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients, originating from Okinawa. This dish is designed to provide an energizing meal option.
instructions: Rinse the Japanese short grain rice thoroughly and soak it in water for 30 minutes.
In a large pot, combine the dashi broth, sesame oil, grated ginger, and powdered turmeric, and bring to a boil.
Add the soaked and drained rice to the pot, stirring gently to combine with the broth mixture.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot with a tight-fitting lid, and simmer for 15 minutes.
While the rice is cooking, crumble the bryndza cheese into small pieces and set aside.
After 15 minutes, fluff the cooked rice with a rice paddle or fork, then stir in the crumbled bryndza cheese.
Transfer the takikomi gohan to a serving platter, garnished with thinly sliced scallions and sesame seeds.
Serve immediately, while the cheese is still melted and the rice is steaming hot.
